generalities.text: Main THE ENCYCLOPEDIA OF PURE MATERIA MEDICA By TIMOTHY F. ALLEN, A.M., M.D. Presented by Médi-T MANCINELLA. Hippomane mancinella, Linn. Natural order , Euphorbiaceæ. Common names , Manchioneal, Manzanillo. Preparation , Tincture of the fruit, leaves, and bark (Bute). Authorities. 1 , Bute, provings with tincture on various persons, from Hering's compilation, All. Zeit. f. Hom., 1850, second supplement, p. 127; 2 , Hering, poisonous effects observed by different persons, ibid.; 3 , Mure, Pathogénésie Brésilienne, p. 156, two provings by E. T. Ackermann, with the tincture (of the leaves); 4 , Ricard-madiana, Recherches et exp. sur les poisons d'Amérique, from Gaz. Hom. de Paris, 1850, p. 406 (see also Am. Observer, March; 1876, same authority, from paper read before Lyceum of Nat. Hist. in New York), effects of chewing the fruit; 4 a , same, effects of volatile poison while digging the roots; 4 b , same, effects of chewing the root; 4 c , same, effects of vapor while preparing a decoction of leaves in water; 4 d , same, effects of taking 4 grains of the gum; 4 e , same, effects of external application of the milky juice; 4 f , same, effects of a few drops of milky juice on tongue; 5 , Peyssonel, Journ. de Méd., 1757 (Frank's Mag., 1, 753), effects of eating two dozen apples; 6 , Kummer, effects of the tincture ( from Hering ); 7 , Rafinesque, general effects ( from Hering ); 8 , Bardill, effects of dew from the leaves dropping on the skin ( from Hering ); 9 , Descourtils, as last; 10 , Castera, from Wibmer, effects of breaking off a small twig; 11 , Rufs, Gaz. Hom. de Paris, 1850, p. 406, pathogenetic effects, from Ann. d'Hyg., 32, 126; 12 , Petroz, symptoms (undistinguished) from provings by self and two women (dose not mentioned), Gaz. Hom. de Paris, 1850, p. 441; 13 , Karsten, having passed some hours under the trees began to experience effects, Tribune Med. (Lond. Med. Rec., 1, p. 752); 14 , Hamilton, ate a very small fragment of the fruit before breakfast, Pharm. Journ., 5, 408; 15 , same, effects on a man of tasting the milky juice; 16 , same, effects of eating the fruit; 17 , same, effects in a negro from eating several of the apples; 18 , same, effects in a man of getting some sap in his eye while felling a tree. MIND. ► Emotional. ► Silent mood (second day), [3] . ► Profound mental tranquillity, in the morning, [3] . ► Cheerfulness; desire to sing (first day), [3] . ► Disposed to take everything in good part (first day), [3] . ► Feelings of tenderness and deep compassion (third day), [3] . ► Sadness (second, fourth, fifth, and seventh days); in morning (sixth day), [3] ; before menses, [12] . ► Anxiety caused by uneasiness in the hypochondria; worse on walking, or becoming chilled, [12] . ► Fearfulness, [1] . ► Morose mood, [1] . [10.] ► Indifference and dulness, in the morning, [1] . ► Intellectual. ► Meditative mood, [12] . ► Activity of mind, disposed to work (ninth day), [3] . ► Aversion to work (fifth day), [3] . ► He is bored by everything (third day), [3] . ► Wandering thoughts (second day), [3] . ► Absence of thought (second day), [3] . ► Great dulness, with indifferent mood, [1] . ► Comatose state; constant drowsiness (third day), [3a] . HEAD. ► Confusion and Vertigo. ► Confusion in the sinciput, lasting eight days, [1] . [20.] ► Confusion in the forehead, [1] . ► Vertigo in the morning, and again at noon, [12] . ► Vertigo, worse in the open air (in a boy), [1] . ► Vertigo on getting out of bed, [12] . ► Vertigo on looking long and steadily at anything, or reading aloud, [12] . ► Vertigo, better in the open air (in a girl), [1] . ► Vertigo, with sensation of torpor, [12] . ► Slight vertigo, with sleepiness, [12] . ► Dizziness, [11] . ► General Head. ► Empty feeling in the head, [12] . [30.] ► Heaviness in the head (twelfth day), [3] . ► Heaviness of the head; he is compelled to lie down, and feels nauseated, [4] . ► Great heaviness of the head, with constant pain in it (third day), [3a] . ► Excessive heaviness of the head (third day), [3a] . ► Mounting from the stomach to the head, and turning around in it, [1] . ► Mounting from the stomach gradually more and more into the head; afterwards vomiting through the nose and mouth, followed by disappearance of the headache, [1] . ► Pain in head, extending to eyes, [12] . ► Pain in the lower part of the head, with heaviness as if it was bruised (second day); the pains in the head continue, and make him impatient (third day), [3] . ► Confused pain in the head when writing (twelfth day), [3] . ► Headache (fifth and sixth days), [3] ; all day, [11] ; (second day), [3a] . [40.] ► The headache becomes intolerable (fourth day), [3a] . ► Headache with vertigo, especially in the morning, after eating a piece of bread (seventh day), [3] . ► Headache, with nausea and soreness beneath the right short ribs; worse while walking, and lasting several hours, [1] . ► Headache, disappearing after vomiting, [1] . ► Incessant headache (third day), [3a] . ► Very violent headache, with very painful lancinations, it continued all night, with sleeplessness (after one hour); continued headache, especially in the temples and above the eyes (second day), [3a] . ► Burning in the head, [12] . ► Congestion to the head, before the menses, [12] . ► Feeling of constriction in the forehead and occiput, [12] . ► Pressure on the brain, [12] . [50.] ► Sticking pressure from the vertex through the brain, at noon, [1] . ► Lancinating pain in the head, as soon as he sits down to eat (twelfth day), [3] . ► Painful lancinations in the head, temples, and above the eyes (third day), [3a] . ► Short stitches in several parts of the head and in the bones of the face, [1] . ► Tearing pain in head, [12] . ► Pain as from a blow, all around the head; after long exposure to the sun (second day), [3] . ► Throbbing in the head, [12] . ► Throbbing in the upper part of the head, [1] . ► Hammering pain in the head and nape of the neck, with inability to bend the head down when writing (fourth day), [3] . ► Forehead. ► Pressure in the forehead, [12] . ► Temples. [60.] ► Pain in the left temple, [1] . ► Lancinating pain in the left temple (fourth day), [3] . ► Sudden sticking in the right temple and above the eyes, [1] . ► Vertex. ► Pain as from a nail driven into the vertex, [12] . ► Pain in the right side of the head, while listening to strokes on an anvil; he felt as if receiving them himself (second day), [3] . ► Parietals. ► Sudden headache in the left side, especially in the temple, [1] . ► External Head. ► Pain in the head, as if the skin of a bladder was applied from temple to temple (twelfth day), [3] . ► Contraction of the scalp at the vertex, [12] . ► Itching on the scalp, [1] . ► Itching on the vertex, at short intervals, [1] . EYE. ► Objective. [70.] ► Blue rings around the eyes, [12] . ► Eyes red and staring, [17] . ► Injection of the vessels of the eyes, [1] . ► The inflammation of the eye ran very high, but was easily subdued; he recovered in three days, [18] . ► Excessively painful inflammation in the eyes, with photophobia, [1] . ► Very violent, painful inflammation in the eyes, so that he feared he would lose his vision; from touching the eye after handling a twig of the plant, [2] . [Relieved by the juice of Bignonia pentaphylla (the Indians use B. leucoxylon)] ► Subjective. ► Burning in the eyes on closing them (at first), [1] . ► Burning in the eyes, which compels him to close them (later), [1] . ► Pressure in the eyes, [1] . ► Sense of weight over the eyes (second day), [3] . [80.] ► Sticking-itching in the right inner canthus, with fluent coryza from the right nostril, [1] . ► The eye symptoms usually alternate with the diminution of hearing, [12] . ► Brow. ► Sticking above the left eye, [1] . ► Lids. ► After some time, the eyelids were swollen to such an extent that they could hardly be opened, and the increased sensitiveness of the eyes was such that he was obliged to shut himself for some days in an absolutely dark room, and even then he still suffered much; the swelling diminished in three days, and the cuticle began to peel off, [13] . ► Feeling of dryness in the lids, [1] . ► Sticking in the middle of the margin of the right lower lid, [1] . ► Ball. ► Cutting pain in the middle of the right eyeball only when he refrains from winking, [1] . ► Dull stitches deep posteriorly in the left eyeball, [1] . ► Vision. ► Objects waver before the eyes, [12] . EAR. ► External. ► Redness and heat of the ears (second day), [3] . [90.] ► Pressure behind the ears, [12] . ► Middle. ► Sensation of numbness in the ears, [12] . ► Twitching in the ears, [12] . ► Itching in the right meatus auditorius, [1] . ► Hearing. ► Roaring in the ears, [1] . ► Ringing in the ears, and whistling as of the wind, when walking (first day), [3] . ► Ringing in the ears, with a drumming sensation, when walking in the wind (fourth day), [3] . ► Noise in the ears, like voices, [12] . NOSE. ► He blows his nose more easily than usual (second day), [3] . ► Sensation of dryness in the nose, [12] . [100.] ► Pressure at the root of the nose, [12] . ► Annoying smells before the nose, like burnt horn, gunpowder, dung, etc., [12] . FACE. ► Red face before a stool, [12] . ► Yellow complexion, with redness of the whole body (second day), [3] . ► Pale face, [11] . ► Pale lips, [12] . ► Drooping of the lower lip, [12] . ► Lancinating pain in the lips, [12] . MOUTH. ► Teeth. ► Sensitiveness of the upper teeth, [12] . ► Tongue. ► Tongue swollen, [17] . [110.] ► Tongue and inside of mouth covered with small pimples, [4] . ► Dry tongue, [12] . ► Burning of the tongue, [1] . ► Very slight prickling on the tongue, [4f] . ► It tingled his tongue gently, [15] . ► General Mouth. ► A blister as large as a hazelnut in the centre of the palate, [4] . ► Blood in the mouth, [12] . ► The palate is excoriated, [12] . ► Inflammation of the mouth, [4] . ► Mucous membrane of mouth filled with small pimples, [4c] . [120.] ► Inside of lips and end of tongue covered with small pimples, [4] . ► Dry mouth (fifth day), [3] . ► Dryness of the mouth, with tenacious mucus, in the morning; offensive odor from the mouth, with coated tongue and thirst (third day), [1] . ► Almost intolerable heat and pricking in the mouth; the burning sensation is not allayed by cold water, [4] . ► Sensation of burning heat in mouth; the smarting was so extreme that I was obliged to hold cold water in my mouth, and renew it frequently (immediately), [4c] . ► Smarting heat on tongue, mouth, and throat (after half an hour) ► Smarting and burning very severe; was obliged to keep cold water in the mouth, the pain becoming insupportable (after two hours), [4b] . ► Mouth feels as if full of pepper, [4] . ► Burning as from pepper, in the mouth and fauces, [1] . ► Pricking in the mouth while eating bread (eleventh day), [3] . ► On account of the state of the mouth and tongue, only liquids can be taken, [4] . ► Saliva. ► Salivation, [11] . [130.] ► Considerable salivation (after two hours), [4b] . ► Copious salivation (after some minutes), [4] . ► Saliva thin and fluid, [15] . ► Burning-hot saliva, [12] . ► Offensive saliva, [12] . ► Mouth filled with water (after a few minutes), [4d] . ► Accumulation of sour water in the mouth, [12] . ► Taste. ► The juice at first tastes flat, then acrid, and causes a burning astringency posteriorly in the fauces (Hering). ► Metallic taste in the mouth (second day), [3] . ► Taste of blood in mouth all day (second day), [3a] . [140.] ► Taste of blood in the mouth, as if from a clot of blood which had risen into the throat (third and fourth days), [3a] . THROAT. ► Great and constant dryness of the throat (second day), [3a] . ► Heat in the throat, along the sophagus, without thirst, [11] . ► Burning, with raw pain, in the throat, the pain gradually extending forward from the arch of the palate, and lasting five hours, [1] . ► The throat feels contracted, [12] . ► Lancination in the throat, [12] . ► (A kind of malignant sore throat), [11] . ► Scraping in the throat, in the region of the larynx, especially sensitive on smoking, [1] . ► Ulcerative pain in throat, [12] . ► Prickling extended to the throat (after adding 3 drops), [4f] . [150.] ► Throat symptoms better when yawning, [12] . ► Fauces, Pharynx, and sophagus. ► Burning in the fauces and root of the tongue, with accumulation of water in the mouth, [1] . ► Burning in the fauces and upper part of the pharynx, [1] . ► Burning in the sophagus, [16] . ► A burning sensation in the fauces, sophagus, and stomach for some hours, [16] . STOMACH. ► Appetite. ► Increased appetite, [12] . ► Great appetite (ninth day), [3] . ► Desire for tobacco, [1] . ► Great desire to smoke (fifth day), [3] . ► Loss of appetite, [1] . [160.] ► Alternate hunger and want of appetite (second day), [3] . ► Almost complete loss of appetite (third day), [3a] . ► Complete loss of appetite (fourth day), [3a] . ► Aversion to everything (sixth day), [3] . ► Loathing (second day), [3] . ► Aversion to bread, [12] . ► Thirst. ► Thirst every hours all day; desire for water, with aversion to wine and all other liquors (fourth day), [3] . ► Constant thirst all day (fifth and seventh days), [3] . ► Increasing thirst until evening (sixth day), [3] . ► Thirst intense, [17] . ► Eructation. [170.] ► Incomplete eructations, [12] . ► Constant eructations of air (first day), [3] . ► Noisy eructations, a kind of sobbing, when food is at all difficult of digestion; it occurs when swallowing, at night, even in sleep, and after drinking beer; also caused by stooping, [12] . ► Hot eructations during expiration, with an oppressed feeling (second day), [3] . ► Nausea and Vomiting. ► Nausea and pain in back during stool, [12] . ► Qualmishness, [1] . ► Vomiting, [11] , [16] . ► Forcible vomiting, [1] . ► Watery vomiting (first day), [3] . ► Vomiting of ingesta, followed by violent griping and copious stools, [4] . [180.] ► Green vomiting, [11] . ► Black vomiting, [1] . ► Bitter vomiting, [1] . ► Stomach. ► Weak stomach (second day), [3] . ► Torpid feeling in the stomach, [12] . ► Severe pain, with weight in the pit of the stomach, for a minute (second day), [3] . ► Sensation of heat in the epigastrium, [11] . ► Burning in the stomach, [16] . ► Burning in the stomach and throat, with nausea, [1] . ► Burning sensation in the stomach, without vomiting, [17] . [190.] ► Its reception in the stomach was attended with a burning sensation, [14] . ► Paroxysmal sticking in the stomach, with diarrha, [1] . ► Epigastric region very sensitive to touch, [1] . ABDOMEN. ► Hypochondria. ► Pain in the hypochondria (fourth day), [3a] . ► Pain in the right hypochondrium, worse when walking, [1] . ► Sudden sticking in the left hypochondrium , [1] . ► Lancinations in the hypochondria and scapulæ (third day), [3a] . ► General Abdomen. ► Distension of the abdomen, [1] . ► After an hour, the abdomen became greatly swollen, while a burning fire raged in his bowels, with trembling of the whole body, cold sweat, weakness, and constant faintness, [5] . ► Increased emission of flatus and empty eructations, [1] . [200.] ► Copious emission of flatus (second day), [3] . ► Pains in the abdomen, [1] . ► Tension of the abdomen, [12] . ► Griping in the bowels (before the menses), [12] . ► Griping at the appearance of the menses, [12] . ► Dull pain in right flank, lasting all day, [12] . ► Transient boring pain in the abdomen on respiration, [12] . ► Twitching pain in the bowels (fourth day), [3] . ► Cutting in the abdomen, followed by diarrha, [1] . ► Lancinating pain in the bowels (fourth day), [3a] . [210.] ► Frequent violent lancinations in the abdomen and bowels (third day), [3a] . ► Repeated violent lancinations in the right side of the abdomen (second day), [3a] . ► Great pain in the abdomen, as if it had been struck with a pointed stick (first day), [3] . ► Colic and diarrha (fourth day), [3] . ► Colic, and four stools, one of them bloody, [11] . ► Hammering pain in the abdomen after breakfast (second day), [3] . ► Bruised pain in the abdominal walls, [12] . ► Sensation as from a blow in the abdomen, followed by an evacuation (seventh day), [3] . ► Hypogastrium and Iliac Regions. ► Pain in the lower portion of the abdomen, followed by a stool, [1] . ► Cramps in the hypogastrium during menstruation, [12] . [220.] ► Griping pain in the right side of the lower abdomen, immediately followed by emission of flatus, [1] . ► Dull pain in the left groin, [1] . ► Lancinations in the groins (fifth day), [3] . RECTUM AND ANUS. ► Swelling of the hæmorrhoids, which become blue, [12] . ► Dark blood from the hæmorrhoids, which coagulates at once, and is offensive, [12] . ► Inertia of the sphincter ani, as if paralyzed, [12] . ► Pain in the anus after stool, [12] . ► A pressive pain in the anus during and immediately following a stool, [1] . ► Pulsation in the anus after stool, [12] . ► Feeling of fulness in the rectum, with a sensation of emptiness in the stomach, [1] . [230.] ► Dragging in the rectum, [1] . ► Urging to stool, [1] . STOOL. ► Diarrha. ► Frequent diarrha, with much emission of flatus; after the diarrha ceased, he was attacked with pains in the legs, from the hips to the ankles, feeling as if the flesh were beaten, [1] . ► Two attacks of diarrha and colic, with twitching pain in the bowels, at midnight (second day), [3] . ► Diarrhic attack, with pain and tenesmus. It began with a natural stool, followed by frequent painful evacuations, at first black and offensive, then watery. Lasted more than two hours (fourth day), [3a] . ► Copious stools, with colic and dizziness, [11] . ► Frequent stools (eleventh day), [3] . ► A great many stools, with discharge of blood, colic, drowsiness, and dizziness, [11] . ► More than twenty stools, from 2 to 8 P.M., with pain and burning heat in the bowels, [4] . ► Thirteen stools, [11] . [240.] ► Nine green stools, without blood, [11] . ► Two stools, [11] . ► Easy stools (second day), [3] . ► Sudden desire for stool in the morning, and entire inability to retain it, followed by tenesmus, [1] . ► Constipation. ► Constipation, [12] . ► Constipation for eight days following the diarrha, [1] . URINARY ORGANS. ► Bladder. ► Considerable excitement in the bladder, [4d] . ► Lancinations in the bladder when beginning to urinate; he feels better while finishing (fourth day), [3] . ► Urethra. ► Burning in the urethra, [1] . ► Micturition. ► Copious micturition, [4d] . [250.] ► Diminished micturition (fourth to sixth day), [1] . ► Urine. ► Urine scanty, whitish (first day); copious, but always whitish (second and third days); clear, copious, and whitish (fourth day); clear and profuse (fifth, sixth, and seventh days), [3] . SEXUAL ORGANS. ► Itching on the scrotum, [1] . ► Sexual desire (fifth day), [3] . ► Menstrual blood pale, [12] . RESPIRATORY ORGANS. ► Larynx. ► Tension of the larynx, [12] . ► Cutting in the larynx, [12] . ► Cough and Expectoration. ► Coughed a little during the day, [4c] . ► Cough caused by irritation of the larynx, [12] . [260.] ► Cough after drinking, [12] . ► Cough, with sweet taste in the throat, [12] . ► Violent cough, with painful pricking in the throat, on the least exertion (fourth day), [3] . ► Cough, with bloody mucous expectoration, [12] . ► The cough is more frequent at midnight, [12] . ► White expectoration (second day), [3] . ► Respiration. ► Slight rattling noise in chest during respiration, [12] . ► Deep and frequent inspirations (fourth day), [3] . ► Difficult breathing when falling asleep again (second day), [3] . ► Suffocation and throbbing in the chest when about to laugh (fourth day), [3] . [270.] ► Sudden suffocation, and violent throbbing in the chest, just as he begins to speak (fourth day), [15] . CHEST. ► Objective. ► The chest is much dilated when drawing breath, even with the mouth closed (third day), [3] . ► Spasmodic contraction of the thoracic walls, [12] . ► Weakness of the chest (fourth day), [3] . ► Subjective. ► Pain in the chest on the least movement (fifth day), [3] . ► Sensation of heat and trembling in the chest (first day), [3] . ► Burning in the chest, [12] . ► Constriction of the chest, [12] . ► Constant dull pain in the left chest, [1] . ► Oppression of chest, [12] . [280.] ► Lancinating pain in the chest (fourth day), [3] . ► Bruised pain in the chest, with difficult breathing (third day), [3] . ► Bruised pain in the clavicles, on turning, the head to either side (twelfth day), [3] . ► Lancination in the sternum, [12] . ► Intense lancinating pains in the left side (third day), [3a] . HEART AND PULSE. ► Præcordium. ► Pain in the præcordial region, after eating, [12] . ► Cramps in the heart, [12] . ► Pricking through the heart (fourth day), [3] . ► Heart's Action. ► Palpitation in the evening, [12] . ► Palpitation after eating, [12] . ► Pulse. [290.] ► Pulse 80, [11] . NECK AND BACK. ► Neck. ► Pain in the nape of the neck and in the forehead, when stooping; it is so confused that he cannot describe it (second day), [3] . ► Hammering pain all around the neck (second day), [3] . ► Hammering pain on the left side of the neck (second day), [3] . ► Hammering pain in the neck, relieved for a few moments by holding the head backwards (eleventh day), [3] . ► Back. ► Crawling as from worms on the right side of the back, close to the middle of the spine, [1] . ► Dorsal. ► Weakness in the dorsal muscles, [12] . ► Rheumatic pain in the shoulder-blade (second day), [3a] . ► Tearing in right shoulder-blade, [12] . ► Lumbar. ► Pain in the region of the kidneys, [1] . [300.] ► Raw pain in the lumbar region, [12] . ► Sacral. ► Boring in the sacral region, [12] . ► Pain in the coccyx, [12] . EXTREMITIES IN GENERAL. ► Lassitude in all the limbs, with indescribable malaise, [4] . ► Feeling of tension in the joints generally, [12] . ► Bruised feeling of the limbs, [11] . SUPERIOR EXTREMITIES. ► Trembling of the arms, [12] . ► Stiffness of the arms, [12] . ► The arm feels swollen, [12] . ► Pressure in the arms, [12] . [310.] ► Lancination in the arms, [12] . ► Three slight beats on the arm, like finger-taps (second day), [3] . ► A sudden electric-like shock through the left arm and shoulder, when writing in the evening, [1] . ► Shoulder. ► Lancinating pain in the left shoulder (fourth day), [3a] . ► Arm. ► Intense lancinating and constrictive pain in the muscles of the upper part of the right arm, for more than an hour (third day), [3a] . ► Elbow. ► Continued pain in the elbow-joint (third day), [3a] . ► Heat in the elbow, [12] . ► Tension in elbow-joint, [12] . ► Very sharp pain in the elbow-joints (second day), [3a] . ► Wrist. ► Pain from morning to night in the wrist and metacarpus, as if strings were tied tight around them (second day); the constrictive pain in the wrist shifts to the middle of the left arm for nearly an hour, after which it returns to the former place (third day), [3] . ► Hand. [320.] ► Redness of the hands during the day (second day), [3] . ► Swelling of the veins of the hands (second day), [3] . ► Twitching in the hands, [12] . ► Awkwardness of the hands, [12] . ► Lancination in the hands, [12] . ► A dull stitch along the metacarpal bone of the left index finger, [1] . ► Fingers. ► Blue finger-nails, [1] . ► A dull stitch in the ball of the left thumb, [1] . ► Tearing pains in the fingers like gout, [15] . INFERIOR EXTREMITIES. ► Objective. ► Trembling of the lower limbs, [11] . [330.] ► Twitching and jerking in both lower extremities, [1] . ► Paralysis, first of the thighs, then of the legs, [1] . ► Subjective. ► Pain up and down the legs, after the diarrha; at last the pain in the left lower extremity disappears and becomes seated on the lower posterior portion of the right thigh, just above the bend of the knee, with a feeling as if a boil would develop, very much impeding walking (repeated after a second dose), [1] . ► Constriction of the thigh and legs, as if a string were tied around them (fifth day), [3] . ► Cramps of the legs and feet, [12] . ► Sudden needle-like stitches in both lower extremities, [1] . ► A bruised sensation in the lower extremities, [1] . ► Thigh. ► Needle-like stitches posteriorly on the thigh, near the hip-joint, [1] . ► Twitching and jerking posteriorly in the middle of the thigh, [1] . ► Knee. ► Lancination in the right knee-joint (third day), [3a] . [340.] ► A sharp stitch as with a needle, in the left patella, somewhat relieved by rubbing, but immediately returning, [1] . ► Pricking for two hours in the left knee-joint (eleventh day), [3] . ► Bruised pain in the bends of the knees (fifth day), [3] . ► Leg. ► The right lower leg falls asleep in the evening, [1] . ► Violent pain like a cramp in the right calf, extending upward to the lower portion of the back, and transversely across the small of the back, with so great weakness that she could scarcely walk, [1] . ► A sore pain on the tibia, in the evening in bed; on applying the hand the pain became a violent burning; next morning there was found a small inflamed spot covered with a crust, [1] . ► Ankle. ► Swelling of the left malleolus (eleventh day), [3] . ► Foot. ► Painful heaviness in the feet, when walking (fifth day), [3] . ► Burning pain on the top of the right foot, as from a glowing coal, [6] . ► Dull pain in the bones of the foot, extending from the heel to the toes, [1] . ► Toes. [350.] ► Repeated dull stitches on the inner side of the left great toe, [1] . GENERAL SYMPTOMS. ► Objective. ► Hands and face swollen and inflamed, [10] . ► Very active when at work, [12] . ► Convulsions, [11] . ► It causes sleep, trembling, convulsions, etc., like narcotic poisons, [7] . ► General debility all day (second, fourth, and fifth days), [3] . ► Prostration (fifth day), [3] . ► Obliged to lie down on account of the fever (which is slight), and quite a bad headache, [4] . ► She is obliged to lie down; with irregular pulse, cold extremities, and the greatest anxiety, [11] . ► He lies down, and gets up alternately (second day), [3] . ► Subjective. [360.] ► Inclination to lie down (sixth day), [3] . ► Inclination to remain lying (eleventh day), [3] . ► General heaviness (sixth day), [3] . ► A nervous sensation of lightness, as if he could soar into the air, [1] . ► Very disagreeable sensation on hearing the noise of a saw (second day), [3] . ► While listening to hammer-strokes, he feels their repercussion all through him (second day), [3] . ► Paralytic sensation immediately after rising in the morning; his hand trembles very much; he cannot open his room-door (second day), [3] . ► Malaise after drinking, [12] . ► Excessive malaise, [11] . ► Lancinations at irregular intervals in the left shoulder-blade, and in the muscles of the left side of the chest (second day), [3a] . [370.] ► Lancination in the muscles of the right knee, also in the ribs and right side (fourth day), [3a] . ► Sticking in the back and on the crest of the ilium, [1] . ► Sudden needlelike stitches here and there , [1] . ► Throbbing in the whole body, [12] . ► Burning in the stomach; swelling of the legs up to the knees, pitting on pressure; red swollen spots as large as the hand here and there on the abdomen and chest; extremely irritable and peevish; began to be better in cool weather; after several weeks there was an eruption of blisters half an inch in diameter, especially on the soles of the feet, when he felt better internally; the swelling remained longest on the ankles (poisoning), [2] . ► Pricking in the skin, together with feverish horripilation; succeeded by heat, with desire to be uncovered; then loss of consciousness; great debility; painful difficulty in swallowing; restlessness; copious urination, [12] . ► Emotional disturbances are accompanied by indescribable uneasiness, hammering pain in the chest, and loss of speech (fourth day), [3] . SKIN. ► Redness of the skin (second day), [3] . ► Red spots on the nose, [12] . ► Yellow spots on the chest, [12] . ► Eruptions, Dry. [380.] ► Small pimples (second day), [3] . ► Eruption of small pimples about the mouth and chin, [12] . ► Many small pimples on the chin; they go off by desquamation, on the third day, [4] . ► Felt a great itching in the part, and perceived a few small red pimples, which occasioned an extreme itching and prickling; they disappear after a few days, without suppuration (second day), [4c] . ► Fine red itching rash on the inner side of the wrist, extending up to the bend of the elbow, [1] . ► Miliary eruption on the face; afterwards the skin is wrinkled, [12] . ► Miliary eruption on the abdomen, [12] . ► Eruptions, Moist. ► Dew falling from the leaves upon the skin causes blisters, [8] . ► It is dangerous to sleep under the tree; the drops of water falling from the leaves cause blisters on the skin, [9] . ► The milky juice burns, draws blisters, inflames, and destroys the hair, [7] . [390.] ► Heat rises to the face; soon followed by smarting, pricking, and burning, which last all day (first day); face swollen, in morning; about noon vesicles appear of the size of a pin's head, and full of yellow serum (second day); the vesicles are absorbed, and desquamation takes place (fourth day), [4a] . ► Herpetic eruption, an inch in diameter, on the right arm; it disappears next day (eleventh day), [3] . ► Eruptions, Pustular. ► When rubbed into the skin it causes a pustular eruption, [2] . ► Subjective. ► Burning in the thighs, and especially in the ankle-joint, [12] . ► A burning sensation over the whole surface of his body; this was presently concentrated on particular points, especially on the face and around the eyes, [13] . ► Raw pain in the skin of the abdomen, [12] . ► Pricking in the feet when sitting (fifth day), [3] . ► Formication in the forehead, [12] . ► Formication in the right hip, with lancinations when walking (seventh day), [3] . ► Continuous formication in both feet at once, especially the left (third day), [3a] . [400.] ► Tickling in the left tibia, of short duration, [1] . ►

Clinical: Asthma; Blindness; Borborygmus; Cholera infantum; Colic; Diphtheria; Eyes, affections of; inflammation of; Hair falling out; Headache; Nervous affections. Œsophagus, stricture of; Scarlatina anginosa; Sore throat; Stomatitis; Tongue, patched; Typhoid fever

Nasal tone of voice. Choking sensation rising into throat hindering speech. In larynx: scraping; cutting; tension. Whistling breathing. Asthma, chronic. Frequent hawking with nausea. Violent cough and painful stitches in trachea from slightest exertion. Cough: night; after drinking. Expectoration > oppression of chest. Icy coldness of hands and feet. Trembling and heaviness of arms; of hands. Hands numb on waking. Painful stiffness of finger-joints. Hands as if too thick; asleep; heavy and clumsy. Blue finger-nails. Quivering and jerking in legs in attacks. Trembling of legs. Needle-like stitches in legs; under heel from within, out. Tingling in feet when sitting. Burning in soles; with dry feeling. Desquamation of soles at end of fever. Large vesicles, esp. on soles. Lassitude and indescribable uncomfortable feeling in limbs; much stretching. Fainting with intestinal colic. Convulsions.

Lassitude and indescribable uncomfortable feeling in limbs. Much stretching, with tired feeling over region of kidneys. Must lie down, pulse irregular, limbs cold, great anxiety. Prostration and trembling in limbs. Fainting with intestinal colic. Convulsions. About midnight, attacks of fear and trembling, followed by feverish heat ; afraid of evil spirits ; of being taken hold of by the devil ; sleeplessness ; only somewhat relieved towards morning ; fears she is getting crazy ; anxious expression in face ; dark brownish circles around eyes ; throbbing in pit of stomach ; pressure in region of heart, hard beats of heart followed by fainty feelings, with darkening before eyes ; pulse slow and soft ; frequent headache ; desire for saltish food ; smell of onions before nose ; menses one week too early, with pains in chest ; feet puffed up every morning ; on chin, old, dark tetter with red pimples. After typhoid fever : frequent shooting pain like a streak down right arm ; constant soreness, extending from region of right kidney into back and around to liver ; sometimes extending down to legs like rheumatism ; head feels heavy, often as if it had been squeezed flat, sometimes as if hollow ; when lying, her head feels as if lying on something hard ; pain over right eye ; feeling of weakness extending from forehead down into nose ; appetite changeable, sometimes hungry without appetite ; after eating, feeling as if hard pieces were lying in stomach, with some nausea ; eating very little makes her immediately very full ; constant desire for sour dishes, particularly for indigestible things (puddings, half baked bread, and the like) ; continual feeling of fulness in stomach, as if food would come up again ; feeling in pit of stomach as of something alive ; raw sensation extending down throat ; speech often weak ; palpitation on turning in bed ; contracting sensation in different parts, mostly of abdomen ; pain in region of bladder while urinating ; menses a few days too late, sometimes very dark, sometimes bright, again greenish and offensive, with tearing pain in lower part of abdomen ; much restlessness ; crawling and digging on different parts of body ; sometimes feels as light as though she could float or hover in the air ; mind much depressed, weeps a great deal, particularly when alone ; full of fear of getting crazy ; > during evening. θ Nervous affection. Periodical : thrusts, as if electric, in upper part of throat. Alternate : diarrhœa and constipation. Every morning : feet puffed up. Right : frequent shooting pain down arm ; soreness extending from kidney into back ; pain over eye. Left : stitches over eye ; diphtheria on side ; rumbling in side when bending to right ; sudden sticking in hypochondrium ; pain in hypochondrium ; lying on right side, rumbling in other ; lying on left side no rumbling ; rattling in chest ; stitches in shoulder blade and breast. From without inward : stitches under heel. As if head was bruised, or as if it had been exposed to sun ; ears feel as if closed ; thrusts, as if electric, in upper part of throat ; as of flames rising from stomach ; as if stomach drew together in a lump and then suddenly opened ; abdominal walls as if bruised ; as if fire was in intestines ; region of kidneys as if bruised ; stitches in heart as from needles ; hands feel as if asleep and numb ; hands as if too thick ; head as if it had been squeezed flat ; head as if hollow ; head as if lying on something hard ; after eating as if hard pieces were lying in stomach ; as if food would come up again ; in pit of stomach as of something alive. Pain : in head ; all over her body, < in chest ; in abdomen ; in region of stomach ; in left hypochondrium in a spot as large as a half dollar ; in intestines ; in centre of sternum ; in chest ; over right eye ; in region of bladder. Severe pain : in middle of sternum. Tearing pain : in lower part of abdomen. Shooting pain : down right arm. Violent burning pain : in throat. Cutting : in abdomen ; in larynx. Stitches : in temples ; in head ; over left eye ; in throat ; in bladder ; painful in trachea ; in left shoulder blade and left breast ; under heel. Needle-like stitches : in lower extremities. Sticking : in various parts of body. Sudden sticking : in left hypochondrium. Pulsating pain : in head and neck. Pricking pains : in lips. Contractions : in opposite directions. Short, sharp stitches : here and there. Wandering pains : in abdomen. Severe colic : in abdomen. Cramps : in legs and feet. Aching : in back. Unendurable burning and prickling : in mouth. Painful itching, stitching and burning : in face. Burning : in eyes ; of eyelids ; on tongue ; at soft palate and roof of mouth ; in ulcers on tonsils and in throat ; in stomach and throat ; in pit of stomach ; in abdomen and anus ; in urethra ; in soles. Soreness : of throat ; of mouth ; of abdominal walls ; in region of kidneys ; from region of kidney into back and around to liver, sometimes down to legs. Heat : of ears ; rising to face ; in pharynx and down œsophagus ; in head. Sore feeling : in whole head. Painfulness : of region of stomach. Raw sensation : extending down throat. Crawling and digging : on different parts of body. Painful stiffness : of neck ; of small of back ; of finger joints. Pressure : at root of nose ; in region of heart. Pressing : in cardiac region. Heaviness : in head ; of eyes and lids ; of arms. Fulness : in rectum ; in stomach. Oppression : of chest. Tension : in larynx. Contracting sensation : in different parts, mostly abdomen. Constriction : in throat, ascending from stomach ; of chest. Choking sensation : rises in throat. Indescribable, uncomfortable feeling : in limbs. Light feeling as though she could float or hover in air. Lightness : in head. Hollow feeling : in stomach. Tired feeling : in region of kidneys. Weakness : of heart ; in region of kidneys ; from forehead into nose. Dryness : of eyes and lids ; of nose ; of tongue ; of mouth ; of throat ; of soles. Tingling : in feet. Trembling : of hands ; of legs ; of limbs. Pulsation : in anus ; in chest. Throbbing : in pit of stomach. Quivering and jerking : in legs. Itching : of scalp ; on scrotum. Icy coldness : of hands ; of feet. Sequela of scarlatina, diphtheria, typhus, etc. TOUCH. PASSIVE MOTION. INJURIES. [45] [Manc.]. Pressure : pit of stomach sensitive ; with hand compelled by pain in left hypochondrium ; pain in middle of sternum <. Inclination to rub region of kidneys : tired feeling. Boy, æt. 12, two years previously had measles and scarlet fever in rapid succession, since then has been sick, and confined much to bed ; affection of stomach. Girl, æt. 13 ; rumbling in abdomen. Young woman, had diphtheria on left side ; post-diphtheritic affections. Woman, æt. 24, unmarried, after typhoid fever ; nervous affection. Mrs. R., æt. 31, dark hair, liver spots in face, mild, somewhat melancholic temperament, disposed to homesickness, mother of six children ; nervous affection. Woman, æt. 40 ; pain in left hypochondrium.

mental_emotional.text: Silent mood, sadness. Wandering thoughts. Sudden vanishing of thought. Bashful. Fear of becoming insane

Sudden vanishing of thought, forgets from one moment to next what she wishes to do. Everything irksome. Fear: of getting crazy; of evil spirits. Averse to work and answering questions. Sadness. Anxiety; before menses. Homesick. Bashful and taciturn; timid look. From anger and after eating.

Sudden vanishing of thought, forgets from one moment to next what she wishes to do. Everything becomes irksome. Fear of getting crazy ; of evil spirits ; of being taken by the devil. Averse to work and answering questions. Melancholy, homesickness ; about midnight attacks of fear and trembling ; afraid of evil spirits, of being taken hold of by the devil ; sleeplessness ; pressing in cardiac region, hard beats of heart, followed by faintishness, with darkening before eyes ; tetters. θ Mental derangement. Bashful and taciturn ; has a timid look. Aggravation from anger and after eating. Vertigo : stupefying ; with loss of consciousness ; in morning on rising from bed. Sensation of lightness in head while walking about room.

head.text: Vertigo; head feels lights, empty. Scalp itches. Hair falls out after acute sickness

Vertigo: stupefying; with loss of consciousness; morning on rising. Sense of lightness (or emptiness) in head while walking about room. Sticking pressure from vertex through brain, at noon. Sudden Sticking in r. temple and above eye. Stitches: in temples; in head with sleeplessness; over l. eye. Headache: with impatience; caused by candle-light; from warmth of stove. Pulsating pains in head and neck, preventing writing. Whole head feels as if bruised, or as after exposure to sun. Itching of scalp; hair falls out after severe acute diseases.

Stitches : in temples ; in head, with sleeplessness ; over left eye. Headache : with impatience ; caused by candlelight. Pulsating pains in head and neck, preventing one from writing. Whole head feels sore, as if bruised or as if it had been exposed to sun. Heaviness in head. Headache, < from warmth of stove. Itching of scalp ; hair falls out after severe acute diseases.

nose.text: Illusions of smell; of gunpowder, dung, etc. Pressure at root of nose
Pressure at root of nose. Dryness.
Pressure at root of nose. Dryness of nose.

mouth_throat.text: Feels peppery. Copious, offensive saliva. Taste of blood. Burning of fauces. Dysphagia from constriction of throat and œsophagus

Very bitter taste. Taste: bloody; after sleep. Tongue: coated white except in several, sharply defined clean spots; coated white as in aphthæ; dry; burning. Unendurable burning and prickling in mouth, not > by cold water. Whole mouth and tongue covered with small vesicles. Saliva: increased; yellow; fetid. Offensive breath, perceptible to herself. Large blisters on palate. Great elongation of uvula. Heat in pharynx and down œsophagus without thirst. Great dryness, on waking; stitches. Choking sensation rises in throat when speaking; prevents drinking though thirsts for cold water. Great swelling and suppurating of tonsils with danger of suffocation; whistling breathing. White, yellowish, burning ulcers on tonsils and in throat.

Very bitter taste in mouth. Tongue coated white, except on several sharply defined clean spots. Burning on tongue ; dry. Tongue coated white as in aphthæ. Taste : bloody ; < after sleeping. Unendurable burning and prickling in mouth, not > by cold water. Whole mouth and tongue covered with small vesicles. Increased flow of saliva ; yellow saliva ; fetid. Burning at soft palate and roof of mouth. Dryness of mouth. Offensive breath, perceptible to herself. Blister, size of hazelnut, on palate. Inflammation in mouth ; bleeds. Great elongation of uvula. Heat in pharynx and down œsophagus, without thirst. Great dryness of throat ; on waking. Choking sensation rises in throat when speaking. Stitches in throat. Sensation of constriction in throat, ascending from stomach, caused by accumulation of air, with palpitation of heart and great sensation of weakness, impeding speech. Thirst for cold water, with inability to swallow on account of a choking which rises up from stomach. Angina following scarlet fever, or during epidemic of latter. Periodical thrusts (as if electric) in upper part of throat, waking from sleep. Yellowish white ulcers, with violent burning pain. Great swelling and suppuration of tonsils, with danger of suffocation ; whistling breathing. Sore throat during scarlet fever. White yellowish burning ulcers on tonsils and in throat. Diphtheria on left side ; ever since, pains all over her body, mostly in chest, with a dry cough. Menses irregular, profuse ; heat in head, with vertigo ; > when at rest ; edges of eyelids burn when closing eyes. θ Post-diphtheritic affection.

stomach_abdomen.text: Continual choking sensation rising from stomach. Vomiting of ingesta, followed by gripping and copious stools. Burning pains and black vomit

Thirst: for cold water; and aversion to wine and alcohol. Aversion to meat and bread. Can take only liquid food on account of soreness of mouth. Drinking water bloatedness and pain in stomach. After drinking water; colic; pain in abdomen. Ineffectual desire to eructate or gag with excessive nausea. Continual choking sensation rising from stomach as pressure of wind, with weakness and palpitation. Repeated green vomit. Sour, greasy vomit with aversion to water; on vomit floats a white mass like coagulated fat. Vomiting of food, followed by severe colic and profuse diarrhœa. Vomiting > headache. Burning: in stomach and throat, with nausea; in pit of stomach. Pit of stomach swollen and sensitive to pressure. Distension and pain in region of stomach. Sensation as of flames rising from stomach. Sensation as if stomach drew together in a lump and then suddenly opened. Rumbling in l. side when bending to r. Sudden sticking in l. hypochondrium. Pain in l. hypochondrium in a spot as large as a florin, compelling her to press there with hand; rumbling in l. side when lying on r., also with every respiration; none when lying on l. side. Wandering pains in abdomen. Pains through bowels after drinking water. Loud rumbling; renewed by every motion and deep breath, with headache which is much near warm stove. Colic: after drinking water; with diarrhœa (at midnight), with fainting, constipation alternating with diarrhœa. Abdomen: distended; sore as if bruised; rash on.

APPETITE, THIRST. DESIRES, AVERSIONS. [14] [Manc.]. Thirst for cold water, but is prevented from drinking by choking sensation rising from stomach ; with burning heat. Aversion to meat and bread. Longing for water and aversion to wine and alcoholic drinks. Can take none but liquid food on account of soreness of mouth. Drinking water, bloatedness and painfulness of region of stomach <. After drinking water : colic ; pain in abdomen. Ineffectual desire to eructate or gag with excessive nausea. Continual choking sensation, rising from stomach like from pressure of wind, with weakness and palpitation of heart. Repeated green vomit. Sour, greasy vomit, with aversion to water ; on vomit floats a white mass like coagulated fat. Vomiting of food, followed by severe colic and profuse diarrhœa. Vomiting relieves headache. Burning : in stomach and throat, with nausea ; in pit of stomach. Pit of stomach swollen and sensitive to pressure. Distension and pain in region of stomach. Bloatedness and painfulness of region of stomach. θ Nervous fever. Sensation as of flames rising from stomach. Sensation as if stomach drew together in a lump and then suddenly opened. Rumbling in left side when bending to right. Sudden sticking in left hypochondrium. Pain in left hypochondrium in a spot as large as a half dollar, compelling her to press there with hand ; rumbling in left side when lying on right side, also with every respiration ; no rumbling when lying on left side. Wandering pains in abdomen. Pains in abdomen through bowels, after drinking water. Loud rumbling in abdomen. Rumbling in abdomen renewed by every motion and when taking a deep inspiration, with headache which is much < near warm stove. Colic : after drinking water ; with diarrhœa (at midnight) ; with fainting, constipation, alternating with diarrhœa. Distended abdomen. Sensation of soreness, as if bruised, of abdominal walls. Eruptions of rash on abdomen.

extremities.text: Icy coldness of hands and feet. Pain in thumb

Trembling and heaviness of arms. When waking, hands feel as if asleep and numb. Trembling of hands. Painful stiffness of finger joints. Hands as if too thick, "asleep", heavy and clumsy. Hands icy cold. Pain and stiffness of finger joints. Blue finger nails. Quivering and jerking in legs in attacks. Trembling of legs. Sudden, needle-like stitches in lower extremities. Cramps in legs and feet. Tingling in feet when sitting. Stitches under heel, from without inward. Burning in soles and dry feeling. Desquamation of soles at end of fever. Large vesicles, especially on soles. Icy coldness of hands and feet.

skin.text: Intense erythema. Vesicles. Fungoid growths. Erysipelas. Large blisters, as from scalds. Heavy, brown crusts and scabs. Pemphigus
Redness of skin (hands). Small vesicles, followed by desquamation. Sequelæ of scarlatina. Favours rapid healing of wounds. Fungoid growths, esp. syphilitic.
Redness of skin (hands). Sequela of scarlet fever. Small vesicles containing yellow serum desquamate following day. Favors rapid healing of wounds. Fungoid growths, particularly if syphilitic.

keynotes.text: color="#800000">MANCINELLA Hippomane-Manganeel Apple Skin symptoms most marked. Dermatitis, with excessive vesiculation, oozing of sticky serum and formation of crusts. To be remembered in mental depressed states at puberty and at climacteric, with exalted sexuality (Hering). Loss of vision. Pain in the thumb.

Manc. is a species of the Euphorbiaceæ, and has all the virulent properties of the family in a high degree. It is one of the trees around which the upas legends have clustered. "It is certain that the juice, which resembles pure white milk, does possess a considerable amount of acridity, and that some persons suffer great pain from incautiously handling it. . . . Perhaps its most dangerous property is that of causing blindness, if by chance the least drop of the milk, or smoke of the burning wood, comes in contact with the eyes. Dr. Seeman states that at Veraguas some of the ship's carpenters were blinded for several days by the juice getting into their eyes while cutting down Manchineel trees; while he himself suffered from temporary loss of sight from merely gathering specimens; and that the same accident happened to a boat's crew from making a fire. Salt water is said to be an efficacious remedy" (Treas. of Bot.). Manc. was introduced into homœopathy by Bute, who proved it. It was also proved by Mure and Ackermann. The symptoms induced were very intense. Burning was experienced in all parts, vesication and ulceration of the skin and mucous membranes. Head, eyes, throat, abdomen and rectum were the parts most affected. A leading feature of many symptoms was by drinking cold water. Cough from drinking water. There is general > from heat when lying down; but warmth of stove headache; and there is inclination to uncover. Cough is at night. Among peculiar sensations are: Head feels as, if bruised; or as if it had been exposed to the sun. Ears as if closed.─Electric thrusts in upper part of throat. As if flames rising from stomach. As if stomach drew together in a lump and then suddenly opened. Head as if hollow; as if lying on something hard. As if something alive in stomach. Med. Cent. (ii. 112) quotes from Revist. Omeop. the case of a lady who for several years was possessed with the fixed idea that she would become insane. She had become a wife and mother, but still the idea possessed her. Happening to see in Puhlmann's Therapeutics that Manc. has "fear of getting crazy," she took Manc. 12 and was permanently cured.

_raw: Compare: Crot. t., jatro., Euphorb. (bot.); Canth. (erythema; vesication of skin; scarlatina); Caps. (burning pains by cold water); Ars.
Compare : Canthar. in erythema and vesiculation of skin, and scarlet fever ; Crot. tig., Yucca filam. and Euphorb. off. have similar skin symptoms

eyes.text: Blue circles round eyes. Intense inflammation causing blindness for some days. Much swelling of lids. Objects waver before eyes. Sensation of heaviness and dryness of eyes and lids. Burning: in eyes, headache from candle-light; of eyelids, only when closing them.
Blue circles around eyes. Sensation of heaviness and dryness of eyes and lids. Burning : in eyes, headache from candlelight ; of eyelids, only when closing them.

ears.text: Ears feel as if closed. Redness and heat of ears. Ringing, roaring, drumming; noise like voices in ears.
Ears feel as if closed. Redness and heat of ears.

face.text: Face: swollen; pale; pale, yellow, bloated. Heat rising to face; soon after, painful itching, stitching, and burning, lasting all day; next morning face swollen; towards noon minute vesicles with yellow fluid; next day, desquamation. Lips pale; pricking pains. Lower lip hangs down. Numerous small vesicles on chin which desquamate.

Face : swollen ; pale ; pale, yellow, bloated. Heat rising to face, and soon thereafter painful itching, stitching and burning, continuing all day, next morning face swollen ; towards noon vesicles, size of small pinhead and filled with a yellow fluid, next day desquamation. Lips pale ; pricking pains in lips. Lower lip hangs down. Large number of small vesicles on chin, which desquamate.

stool_rectum.text: Fulness in rectum with hollow feeling in stomach. Profuse diarrhœa with colic and vertigo. Many bloody stools with colic, inclination to sleep, and vertigo. Greenish stools without blood. Diarrhœa: with burning in abdomen and anus; alternating with constipation; with cutting in abdomen and much discharge of flatus. Stools: frequent, thin, watery, with vomiting; painful, black, fetid, bloody, with tenesmus. After stool: pulsation in anus, discharge of fetid blood from piles. Constipation.

Fulness in rectum, with a hollow feeling in stomach. Profuse diarrhœa, with colic and vertigo. Many bloody stools, with colic, inclination to sleep and vertigo. Greenish stools, without blood. Diarrhœa : with burning in abdomen and anus ; in alternation with constipation ; with cutting in abdomen ; with much discharge of flatus. Sudden desire to go to stool (in morning), after stool tenesmus. Bloody evacuations, with colic, sleepiness and giddiness. Stools : painful, black, fetid, bloody, with tenesmus. During afternoon, more than twenty evacuations, with pain in intestines as if fire was there. Sensation of fulness in rectum. After stool : pulsation in anus ; discharge of fetid blood from hemorrhoidal tumors. Frequent thin, watery discharges ; vomiting ; emaciation ; great prostration ; loss of appetite ; aversion to food. θ Cholera infantum. Constipation.

urinary.text: Tired feeling in region of kidneys (as if bruised) with inclination to rub parts, and frequent stretching back of body. Stitches in bladder before and at beginning of urination. Burning in urethra. Urine: brown; whitish, clouded on standing.

Tired feeling in region of kidneys, with inclination to rub parts, and with frequent stretching back of body. Sensation of weakness, as if bruised, in region of kidneys. Stitches in bladder before and at beginning of micturition. Burning in urethra. Urine : brown ; whitish, clouded on standing.

male.text: Increased sexual desire. Itching on scrotum.
Increased sexual desire. Itching on scrotum.

female.text: Pale menstrual flow; colic. Before menses: congestion of head.
Pale menstrual flow ; colic. Before menstruation : congestion of head.

chest_heart.text: Constriction of chest. Rattling in l. chest. Suffocative attacks with pulsation in chest while coughing, or as soon as he begins to talk. Pain in centre of sternum, from pressure and from breathing. Needle-like stitches in heart. Palpitation.

Constriction of chest. Oppression of chest ; > after expectoration. Rattling in left chest. Suffocative attacks, with pulsation in chest, while coughing, or as soon as he begins to talk. Stitches in middle of sternum. Pain in centre of sternum, < from pressure and from breathing. Stitches in heart as from needles. Palpitation of heart : in evening, as from eating ; weakness. Pulse : weak and somewhat accelerated ; 80. Stitches in middle of sternum ; < from breathing.

back.text: Painful stiffness of neck, esp. after sleep. Stitches in l. scapula and l. breast at intervals. Painful stiffness in small of back.

Painful stiffness of neck, especially after sleep, could scarcely move neck. Stitches in left shoulder blade and left breast at intervals. Backache with nausea and tenesmus. Soreness in region of kidneys. Painful stiffness of small of back. Tired sensation in region of kidneys, desire to rub part ; much stretching. Painful stiffness of small of back and of finger joints which she could scarcely bend.

sleep.text: Great sleepiness; ineffectual desire to yawn. Wakes as from electric shocks on neck (above larynx). On awaking, hands asleep, heavy. After sleep: mouth bitter; throat dry; neck stiff.

Great sleepiness ; ineffectual desire to yawn. Wakes as from electric shocks on neck (above larynx). When awaking hands "asleep" and heavy, and feel as if too thick. After sleep : bitter mouth ; dryness in throat ; could scarcely move neck.

fever_chill.text: Chill: icy cold hands and feet. Flushes of heat, with sensation as if flames rose out of region of stomach, on account of which he threw off clothes. Burning heat, with inclination to cover. Moderate fever, severe headache, must lie down. Cold sweat. Fever with tingling of skin, desire to uncover, painful swelling, profuse micturition. Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Main.

Chill ; icy cold hands and feet. Flushes of heat, with sensation as if flames rose out of region of stomach, on account of which threw off covering. Burning heat, with inclination to cover himself in bed ; with thirst. Moderate fever, severe headache, must lie down. Cold sweat. Coldness of hands and feet. Fever heat with tingling in skin and desire to uncover one's self, with loss of consciousness, painful deglutition, restlessness, profuse micturition. Typhoid fever with tympanitis and sensitiveness of abdomen, < by drinking water.

respiratory.text: VOICE AND LARYNX. TRACHEA AND BRONCHIA. [25] [Manc.]. Nasal tone of voice. Choking sensation rising up into throat, hindering speech. In larynx : scraping ; cutting as with a knife ; tension. Whistling breathing. During breathing : rattling in left chest ; loud rumbling in abdomen ; severe pain in middle of sternum, < from pressure. Asthma, chronic. Frequent hawking, with nausea. Violent cough and painful stitches in trachea from slightest exertion. Cough : < at night ; after drinking. Expectoration relieves oppression of chest.

modalities.text: REST. POSITION. MOTION. [35] [Manc.]. Rest : heat in head >. Lying : head feels as if lying on something hard. Must lie down : pulse irregular, limbs cold, great anxiety ; severe headache. Sitting : tingling in feet. Frequent stretching back : of body, tired feeling of region of kidneys. Every motion : renews rumbling in abdomen. Slightest exertion : violent cough and painful stitches in trachea. Could scarcely move neck : stiffness. Walking : about room ; lightness in head. Morning : on rising, vertigo ; sudden desire to go to stool ; attacks of fear >. Towards noon : vesicles, size of small pinhead, on face, next day desquamation. All day : painful itching, stitching, burning in face. Afternoon : more than twenty evacuations. Evening : palpitation of heart ; nervous affection >. Night : cough <. Midnight : attacks of fear, trembling, diarrhœa and colic. Warmth of stove : headache <. Inclination to uncover himself : burning heat. Cold water : does not > prickling and burning in mouth. Amelioration from heat and when lying down.
